Restaurants & Dining

Find dining that adds context to the trip, not just a meal stop

Use destination-aware dining recommendations to help travelers understand local food culture, lodge dining, and practical places to eat between activities.

Dining Directory

Food helps travelers understand the destination better

The best travel pages do more than list attractions. They show where a traveler might eat well, slow down, and get a more grounded sense of place.

Reset
14 dining listings available
Kenya Midrange

East Africa Ģ Kenyan grill and farm-to-table

Mara Fireside Table

Bush-facing dining with nyama choma, seasonal greens, and sundowner service after game drives.

Sunset tasting menu
Open destination page ?
Uganda Midrange

East Africa Ģ Ugandan regional and grill

Nile Deck Kitchen

Relaxed lodge dining near the river with grilled tilapia, matoke, and sunset views after the falls cruise.

Nile tilapia supper
Open destination page ?
Uganda Midrange

East Africa Ģ Farm-to-lodge cuisine

Bwindi Forest Table

A quiet forest-edge dining room serving produce-driven menus built for trekkers returning from the hills.

Trek recovery lunch
Open destination page ?
Kenya Midrange

East Africa Ģ Kenyan lodge dining

Kilimanjaro View Grill

Open-air dinners with Kilimanjaro views, slow breakfasts, and a strong base for family safari evenings.

Bush breakfast with mountain view
Open destination page ?
Tanzania Midrange

East Africa Ģ Swahili seafood and spice-forward plates

Stone Town Rooftop Kitchen

A relaxed Stone Town rooftop for seafood, spice-rich curries, and sunset views over old coral-stone rooftops.

Spice market seafood platter
Open destination page ?
Tanzania Midrange

East Africa Ģ East African comfort food

Serengeti Salt Kitchen

Relaxed lodge restaurant known for fresh tilapia, chapati breakfasts, and easy family dinners between drives.

Swahili breakfast board
Open destination page ?
South Africa Midrange

Southern Africa Ģ Cape Malay and modern South African

Cape Quarter Pantry

A design-forward city stop for spice-rich plates, natural wine, and neighborhood storytelling.

Cape Malay tasting supper
Open destination page ?
Zimbabwe Midrange

Southern Africa Ģ Regional plates and riverside cocktails

Falls Market Kitchen

A lively pre- or post-activity stop for grilled fish, local produce, and Zambezi sundowners.

Zambezi sunset dinner
Open destination page ?
Morocco Budget

North Africa Ģ Berber home-style cooking

Desert Lantern Supper Club

Campfire dinners with tagines, mint tea, and live drumming under a clear desert sky.

Tagine under the stars
Open destination page ?
Rwanda Luxury

Central Africa Ģ Rwandan produce-led dining

Volcanoes Harvest Room

Elegant post-trek dinners focused on seasonal vegetables, coffee, and mountain views.

Chef-led gorilla trek recovery menu
Open destination page ?
Namibia Luxury

Southern Africa Ģ Desert lodge dining

Namib Ember Table

A lodge dining room built around sunset dinners, stargazing pauses, and slow Namib evenings.

Desert dusk tasting dinner
Open destination page ?
Egypt Midrange

North Africa Ģ Egyptian classics and terrace dining

Pyramids Lantern Dining Room

A practical Cairo-Giza dining stop for travelers who want local classics and evening pyramid views.

Cairo mezze supper
Open destination page ?
Morocco Midrange

North Africa Ģ Moroccan riad dining

Marrakech Courtyard Table

An atmospheric courtyard dining room for tagines, salads, mint tea, and slower evenings after medina walking.

Classic riad dinner
Open destination page ?
Ghana Midrange

West Africa Ģ Ghanaian coastal cuisine

Cape Coast Verandah Kitchen

A relaxed coastal dining stop built around fish, stews, and easy evenings after heritage touring.

Coastal fish supper
Open destination page ?

Trek Africa Guide

A minimalist but comprehensive Africa travel directory built to help travelers discover where to go, what to do, where to stay, and where to eat before they book with verified partners.
